answer: The correct answer is \boxed{H}. Roman numerals are used in naming compounds when the cation can have multiple oxidation states, such as copper in CuCl₂ (copper(II) chloride). The other options involve cations with fixed charges (e.g., Al³⁺, Na⁺, K⁺, Ca²⁺, NH₄⁺, Mg²⁺, Zn²⁺) or are not ionic compounds in the same naming context.
